posts.author: firefueled
posts.community: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd : A metal recycling compounder in the making
posts.text: The main pain points were in Q4 FY26 , where EBITDA margin dropped to about 3.5% despite strong revenue growth. The biggest hit came from a ₹18,000–18,500 per ton compression in copper realization formulas, plus another ₹6,000 per ton impact from higher logistics, liner, port discharge, and fuel costs linked to regional disruptions. There was also a working-capital strain , which pushed operating cash flow negative even in a strong year. Management said this was driven by higher inventory needs from price and volume growth, along with accounting-related presentation changes that made receivabl…

posts.community: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd : A metal recycling compounder in the making
posts.text: More than margins, cash flows should be looked into, this year they turned negative. Hope company will respond in the earnings call.

posts.author: RocketMan
posts.community: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd : A metal recycling compounder in the making
posts.text: Not across the industry, only for those who were relied on gulf for scrap sourcing, other companies who had more diversified sourcing have done exceedingly well this quarter.

posts.author: karanshah137
posts.community: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd : A metal recycling compounder in the making
posts.text: Across the industry, processing margins have faced immense pressure, forcing companies to rely purely on premium products or volume expansions rather than high margins per ton. The topline is high because of the high-volume, lower-margin product mix. They are currently pushing heavy volumes of basic copper ingots to capture market share and feed their massive ongoing capex, sacrificing near-term margins for topline scaling. This margin dip is likely in a transitional phase because input scrap costs rose faster than selling prices. The margin story will likely only structurally turn around once…

posts.author: YashSodha
posts.community: Jain Resource Recycling Ltd : A metal recycling compounder in the making
posts.text: Q4 results are out. Not sure how to read through the results as while topline growth continues there is a significant decline in margins (QoQ). Based on segmental results, copper and copper ingots division seems to be the sole culprit. Not sure why did the margins decline there when copper prices are hitting new highs daily. bseindia.com 8ea95c49-d937-42bb-9bce-6bf52a44356f.pdf
